commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d6a73d5830ac521a980e6364fbd7693c048df5d4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR rounds compact thousands and millions to nearest values, carries rounded thousands into millions, simplifies decimal formatting, and adds six formatter tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main deterministically truncates affected ranges through integer division, although this read-only review did not launch the macOS app.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The arithmetic repair is narrow, but the mandatory real behavior proof must come from the contributor's setup and cannot be supplied by an automated repair worker.

target.title: fix: round compact stat badges to nearest instead of truncating
target.body:
## Summary

`StatValueFormatter.compact` rounds counts under 10K to the nearest value (`1999 → "2K"`), but counts in the 10K–1M and 10M–1B ranges were **truncated** by integer division (`value / 1000`), so badges under-reported and broke from both the formatter's own sub-10K rounding and GitHub's compact display.

| value | before | after |
|------:|:------:|:-----:|
| 10,500 | `10K` | `11K` |
| 19,999 | `19K` | `20K` |
| 99,500 | `99K` | `100K` |
| 567,890 | `567K` | `568K` |
| 999,500 | `999K` | `1M` |
| 10,500,000 | `10M` | `11M` |

This rounds every range to nearest, carries `999,500 → "1M"` so a rounded thousands value never renders as `1000K`, and keeps the existing `999M` cap for oversized inputs. It also drops a dead `hasPrefix("10")` branch in `oneDecimal` — the `.0` suffix check already collapses the `9.95 → 10.0` case, so that branch never executed.

Affects every badge routed through `compact`: issue, PR, star, fork, comment, and release **download** counts (the last commonly reach the ranges that were truncating).

## Tests

Adds `StatValueFormatterTests` (Swift Testing) covering pass-through, sub-10K decimals, thousand/million rounding, the carry into `M`, and the oversized cap. No screenshot — rendering is unchanged; only the formatted value differs (see table).

## Commands run

- `swift build --target RepoBar` — clean
- `swift test --filter StatValueFormatterTests` — 6/6 pass
- `swiftformat --lint <changed files>` — no changes needed
- `swiftlint lint --strict <changed files>` — no violations
